phrases.summary: The text states that Asaph produced a son named Jehoshaphat (i.e., Jehoshaphat was born from Asaph).
The text states that Jehoshaphat produced a son named Joram (i.e., Joram was born from Jehoshaphat).
The text states that Joram produced a son named Uzziah (i.e., Uzziah was born from Joram).

phrases.phrase: Ἀσὰφ δὲ ἐγέννησεν τὸν Ἰωσαφάτ, Ἰωσαφὰτ δὲ ἐγέννησεν τὸν Ἰωράμ, Ἰωρὰμ δὲ ἐγέννησεν τὸν Ὀζίαν,
phrases.translation: And Asaph begot Jehoshaphat, and Jehoshaphat begot Joram, and Joram begot Uzziah.
phrases.summary: Together, the clauses describe a direct father-to-son lineage: Asaph is presented as the father of Jehoshaphat, Jehoshaphat as the father of Joram, and Joram as the father of Uzziah.
